title: ajuga chamaepitys subsp. cuneatifolia
intro: Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. cuneatifolia, also known as bugle, is a subspecies of the Ajuga genus of flowering plants. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. cuneatifolia include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in wound healing, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. cuneatifolia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in tincture or capsule form.
